page.primary_keyword: New Year's Eve yacht charter Marbella 2027
page.title: New Year's Eve 2027: Yacht Charter in Marbella with Fireworks

data.meta_description: Book a New Year's Eve yacht charter Marbella 2027 with fireworks views from Puerto Banús. From €749/2h with skipper, drinks & snacks included.
data.summary: Celebrate New Year's Eve 2027 aboard a private yacht in Marbella. Watch the fireworks from the water with a fully crewed charter from Puerto Banús, including drinks and snacks.
key_facts: Charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39)
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, insurance & Spanish VAT
Complimentary drinks: water, soft drinks, beer, white wine & cava
Light snacks provided on every booking
Fireworks displays visible from the bay at midnight
Max capacity ranges from 5 to 12 passengers per boat
Book early – New Year's Eve slots fill quickly

faq.q: What is included in a New Year's Eve yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Spanish VAT (21%) is also included. You just need to bring your party spirit.

faq.q: Which boats are available for New Year's Eve 2027?
faq.a: Our fleet ranges from the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) at €749/2h to the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). Tier A boats start at €749/2h; Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 require a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Can I see the Marbella fireworks from the yacht?
faq.a: Yes. Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina host midnight firework displays visible from the bay. Your skipper will position the yacht for the best view, away from the crowds.

faq.q: How many people can join the charter?
faq.a: Capacity varies by boat: from 5 passengers on the Dubhe or Mariah SX21 up to 12 passengers on larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94. Check the specific boat page for exact limits.

faq.q: What time should I book for New Year's Eve?
faq.a: We recommend a 3-4 hour charter starting around 9:30 PM to enjoy the sunset, cruise the coast, and anchor for the midnight fireworks. Shorter 2-hour trips are possible but may feel rushed.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.a: Our charters include drinks and snacks, but you are welcome to bring extra food or celebratory drinks. No glass bottles on board – use plastic or cans.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for New Year's Eve 2027?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. Early booking is advised – most popular boats are reserved by October. Provide your preferred boat, group size, and start time.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety comes first. If conditions are unsafe, we offer a full refund or reschedule to an alternative date. The skipper makes the final call.

data.body_html:
New Year's Eve in Marbella is a spectacle of light and celebration. The glittering marina of Puerto Banús, the silhouette of La Concha mountain, and the midnight fireworks over the Mediterranean create an unforgettable backdrop. A New Year's Eve yacht charter Marbella 2027 lets you watch it all from the water – with a glass of cava in hand, no crowds, and your own private party on deck.

Why charter a yacht for New Year's Eve in Marbella?

Fireworks from the shore mean packed promenades, limited visibility, and long walks back to your car. On a private yacht, you anchor in the bay with an unobstructed view of the displays at Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina. Your crew handles navigation, drinks, and snacks. You simply enjoy the countdown with friends or family.

The Costa del Sol in late December averages 16-18°C – cool enough for a jacket, mild enough for an evening on deck. Charters typically depart between 9:30 PM and 10:00 PM, giving you time to cruise the coastline before anchoring for the midnight show.

Our fleet for New Year's Eve 2027

We offer boats for every group size and budget. All depart from Puerto Banús. Prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

BoatLengthMax PaxPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749€2,299
Azimut 3912.5m11€749€2,299
Mangusta 80 (all variants)24m12€4,719 (4h min)N/A
Other boats8m-29m5-12Pricing on request via WhatsApp

Popular choices for larger groups include the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) and the Maiora 26m (26m, 12 pax). For an intimate evening, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) offer a cosy setting.

What's included on every New Year's Eve charter

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the full duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts, or similar)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

No hidden costs. No tips required (though appreciated). Just show up at Puerto Banús and step aboard.

Best spots to watch the fireworks from the water

Your skipper will anchor in one of two prime locations:

  • Puerto Banús bay – directly in front of the marina, with the fireworks reflecting off the superyachts.
  • Marbella Marina – quieter, with a view of the Golden Mile and the town's display.

Both spots are within a 10-minute cruise from our base. If you book a longer charter, you can also cruise past Cala del Faro or Cabopino before returning for midnight.

How to prepare for your New Year's Eve yacht charter

  • Dress in layers – a warm jacket or fleece is recommended for after sunset.
  • Bring non-glass bottles if you want extra drinks (glass is not allowed on board).
  • Arrive 15 minutes early at Puerto Banús for boarding.
  • Let us know if you have dietary requirements for snacks.
  • Book early – most boats are reserved by late October.

Pricing and booking for New Year's Eve 2027

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) are the most affordable option at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. For a premium experience, the Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 and includes a jet ski. All other boats are priced on request – simply message us on WhatsApp with your preferred boat and group size.

We recommend a 3-4 hour charter for New Year's Eve: enough time to cruise, enjoy sunset at 5:15 PM, anchor for fireworks, and return by 1:00 AM. A 2-hour charter works but feels rushed.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in a New Year's Eve yacht charter in Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Spanish VAT (21%) is also included. You just need to bring your party spirit.

Which boats are available for New Year's Eve 2027?

Our fleet ranges from the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) at €749/2h to the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). Tier A boats start at €749/2h; Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 require a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Can I see the Marbella fireworks from the yacht?

Yes. Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina host midnight firework displays visible from the bay. Your skipper will position the yacht for the best view, away from the crowds.

How many people can join the charter?

Capacity varies by boat: from 5 passengers on the Dubhe or Mariah SX21 up to 12 passengers on larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94. Check the specific boat page for exact limits.

What time should I book for New Year's Eve?

We recommend a 3-4 hour charter starting around 9:30 PM to enjoy the sunset, cruise the coast, and anchor for the midnight fireworks. Shorter 2-hour trips are possible but may feel rushed.

Can I bring my own food or drinks?

Our charters include drinks and snacks, but you are welcome to bring extra food or celebratory drinks. No glass bottles on board – use plastic or cans.

How do I book a yacht for New Year's Eve 2027?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. Early booking is advised – most popular boats are reserved by October. Provide your preferred boat, group size, and start time.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety comes first. If conditions are unsafe, we offer a full refund or reschedule to an alternative date. The skipper makes the final call.
